feature_name: Dual-mode operation (Root Complex or End Point)
description: Module supports operation as Root Complex or End Point. Mode selection controls configuration responsibilities and enumeration behavior. RC initiates configuration cycles while EP exposes Type 0 configuration space to a host. Mode must be finalized before link training to avoid undefined behavior. This feature is central to bring-up policy and software stack selection.
dependencies: PSC power enabled; DEVSTAT PCIe enable; valid SerDes refclk; configure before LTSSM enable
Remarks: Select the role early at boot and keep it constant until reset.

description: The link supports 2.5 GT/s (Gen1) and 5.0 GT/s (Gen2). Negotiation occurs during training under software policy. Use conservative defaults during bring-up and enable higher speed after stability is verified. Speed changes should be coordinated with PHY/equalization readiness. Always verify negotiated speed after training.
dependencies: Stable reference clock; SerDes PLL lock; common-clock/SSC settings consistent
Remarks: Start at Gen1 for stability, then switch to Gen2 once validated.

feature_name: Link width x1/x2 with lane reversal and polarity inversion
description: The controller can operate with one or two lanes depending on board design. Automatic lane reversal and polarity inversion permit flexible routing. Down-training allows operation when fewer lanes are detected. Software should read status to confirm the active width. Validate board routing and SerDes settings against intended operation.
dependencies: SerDes lane enable configured; partner compatibility; PLL locked
Remarks: Allow auto-detect unless board constraints require forcing specific settings.

description: Outbound windows map internal addresses to PCIe memory or I/O spaces. They are programmed before enabling and used for CPU or DMA initiated TLPs. Accesses should be performed only after the link is up to avoid hangs. Sizes and alignments must match target requirements. Improper mappings can cause decode errors or timeouts.
dependencies: Program OB indices/size first; enable after config; link up required
Remarks: Validate targets and guard accesses with link-up checks.

description: Inbound regions expose internal memory to the PCIe side. They are tied to BARs and define host-visible apertures. Proper sizing and alignment are required for interoperability. Enable only after BARs are negotiated. Careful scoping limits attack surface and simplifies decode.
dependencies: BAR sizing and host enumeration alignment; enable inbound translators
Remarks: Use minimal apertures consistent with performance goals.

description: The module supports selecting application endianness. Configuration must match the system architecture to avoid data corruption. Set this before enabling translators or traffic. Changes after traffic starts require quiescence. Consistent interpretation across host and device prevents mismatches.
dependencies: Program endianness prior to OB/IB enable and DMA
Remarks: Audit all MMIO and DMA paths for correct byte ordering.

feature_name: TLP attributes: Relaxed Ordering and No Snoop
description: Outbound TLPs may be tagged with Relaxed Ordering and No Snoop. These attributes can improve throughput on tolerant fabrics. They must only be used when memory consistency requirements permit. Some partners ignore or limit them. Measure system impact before enabling broadly.
dependencies: System memory ordering and cache policy must allow; measure impact
Remarks: Enable selectively for large sequential DMA transfers.

description: Legacy INTA/B/C/D signaling is supported for compatibility. These signals are mapped to SoC interrupt lines. Proper masking and clearing sequences are required for correctness. Latency may be higher than MSI under load. Use as a fallback when MSI is unavailable.
dependencies: SoC interrupt routing; status/enable sequencing; EOI writes
Remarks: Prefer MSI for performance; retain INTx for bring-up and fallback.

description: As Root Complex the controller receives MSI from endpoints. Vectors are enabled and serviced in software with write-one-to-clear semantics. Route only the vectors in use to minimize overhead. Ensure endpoints enable MSI during enumeration. Coalesce handling where appropriate for rate control.
dependencies: Enumeration must enable MSI on endpoints; SoC IRQ mapping
Remarks: Batch-handle vectors per group to reduce register traffic.

description: As End Point the controller can generate MSI to signal events to the host. Software programs the local capability and triggers an interrupt when needed. Host allocation and enablement of vectors is required for operation. Order signaling after the associated work is visible to the host. Use MSI in preference to legacy INTx when supported.
dependencies: MSI capability configured; host assigned vectors; valid BAR/IB regions
Remarks: Coalesce interrupts to balance latency and CPU overhead.

description: The controller supports device power management states. Active State Power Management allows L0s and L1 link states for energy savings. Entry and exit should occur with quiesced traffic. Latency impacts must be considered for real-time paths. L2 is not supported and should not be used.
dependencies: Stable link; partner support; follow timing for transitions
Remarks: Enable ASPM only after validating clocking and latency budgets.

description: Dedicated interrupts are provided for power management and reset events. Software must enable, service, and clear status to maintain responsiveness. Proper ordering ensures causes are captured before clearing. These signals coordinate orderly shutdown and recovery. Neglect can stall transitions or miss events.
dependencies: SoC IRQ mapping; correct status clear order
Remarks: Log PM and reset causes for diagnostics and policy tuning.

description: Advanced Error Reporting extends visibility into correctable and fatal errors. End-to-end CRC improves detection of TLP corruption. Enable reporting and capture logs before clearing to preserve root cause. Some partners may not fully implement AER and require testing. Integrate counters into system health monitoring.
dependencies: AER capability present; interrupts routed; log buffers available
Remarks: Trigger recovery flows on fatal errors and alert system management.

description: SerDes PLL must be programmed and locked before link training. Poll lock status and respect stabilization delays. Lane-specific status helps isolate signal integrity issues. Configuration may require a device-specific unlock sequence. Proceed only after stable lock is confirmed.
dependencies: Stable refclk; kicker/unlock if required; observe delays
Remarks: Abstract PLL programming into a reusable routine shared across platforms.

feature_name: Link training control and LTSSM monitoring
description: Software controls enabling of the link training state machine. Training should start only after configuration is ready. Status should be monitored until the operational state is reached. Timeouts and retries improve robustness on marginal links. Training failures usually indicate clocking, cabling, or configuration issues.
dependencies: SerDes locked; translators configured; link event interrupts set
Remarks: Gate enumeration on successful L0 detection with bounded retries.

description: A software-initiated hot reset can be issued to downstream devices. Traffic must be quiesced prior to assertion. Adequate delays are required before resuming transactions. Proper handling ensures clean re-enumeration. Misuse can lead to timeouts or link hangs.
dependencies: RC role; traffic flushed; reset timing observed
Remarks: Integrate hot reset into recovery flows with backoff policies.

description: The Root Complex can issue configuration and I/O cycles via dedicated windows. Accesses must be single 32-bit, non-burst operations. Attempts with the link down or missing targets can hang the bus. Correct targeting between Type 0 and Type 1 is required. Guard all operations with link and presence checks.
dependencies: CFG_SETUP programmed; IOBASE set; target presence verified
Remarks: Prefer memory space over I/O where possible for modern endpoints.

description: Inbound and outbound translators are gated by enable bits. This allows staged configuration and safe bring-up. Quickly clearing enables isolates faults during debug. Disable translators before performing major remaps. Sequencing reduces exposure to malformed or misrouted traffic.
dependencies: OB/IB tables valid; known link state; handlers ready
Remarks: Centralize translator state in feature flags for predictable control.

description: Application-space registers expose fine-grained PCS and SerDes controls. These are valuable for signal integrity bring-up and margining. Unvalidated changes can impact stability in production. Access should be limited and well-documented. Status helps diagnose lane readiness and enablement.
dependencies: SerDes powered; kicker unlock as needed
Remarks: Hide expert-only knobs behind guarded build options.

description: The module interfaces to the SoC with wide internal buses for high throughput. Special controller address spaces require strict access rules. Software must use 32-bit, non-burst, non-cacheable MMIO for these regions. Violations can cause protocol errors or deadlocks. DMA paths should use normal memory-mapped windows, not controller register spaces.
dependencies: MMIO attributes set to device/strongly-ordered; use helpers
Remarks: Provide MMIO helpers that enforce width and ordering rules.

description: When a common reference clock is shared, software should enable the common-clock setting. Spread-spectrum clocking can reduce EMI if both ends support it. Mismatched settings degrade link stability, especially at higher speeds. Proper configuration maximizes interoperability. Always confirm negotiated settings in link status.
dependencies: Board clock topology known; SerDes/PL GEN2 configured
Remarks: Document clock topology per platform and validate in manufacturing tests.

description: A structured sequence brings the controller to operational RC state. Power and configure SerDes before attempting link training. Program endianness, translators, and interrupts with training disabled. Enable training, wait for the operational state, then enumerate and enable MSI. Include timeouts and retries to handle slow devices and recovery.
dependencies: PSC enabled; SerDes lock; timeouts/retries for robustness
Remarks: Automate bring-up with checkpoints and error handling.

description: End Point bring-up configures BARs, capabilities, and inbound windows prior to training. SerDes must be configured and locked before enabling the state machine. After link-up the host performs configuration cycles. MSI should be enabled if supported and mapped. Defer DMA until host completes configuration and memory windows are validated.
dependencies: BAR sizes selected; IB/OB programmed; SerDes lock
Remarks: Service host requests promptly and validate assigned apertures.

description: Hardware hot-plug support is not provided by this controller. The L2 link state is not supported and should not be used. Software should avoid relying on these features. Power policies must target supported link states only. System-level solutions may be needed for device presence changes.
dependencies: Design policies avoid L2/hot-plug; use alternatives
Remarks: Reflect these limits in platform documentation and policies.

feature_name: Payload and read request size limits
description: The controller enforces maximum payload and request sizes. Outbound payloads are limited to a lower size than inbound. Negotiated sizes must be programmed consistently during enumeration. Descriptor design and batching should align with these limits. Violations can reduce performance or cause rejections.
dependencies: Enumeration negotiates MPS/MRRS within limits
Remarks: Tune DMA chunking to align with negotiated sizes and IOMMU boundaries.

description: Inbound transactions can carry priority and privilege identifiers. These integrate with SoC fabric QoS policies. Misconfiguration could starve or overload other masters. The feature is optional but useful in shared-memory environments. Values should be audited in safety-critical systems.
dependencies: Align with SoC QoS/security policy
Remarks: Expose conservative tunables via platform configuration.

description: Activity and diagnostic registers provide insight during operation. They assist with performance tuning and troubleshooting. Intrusive diagnostics can perturb timing and should be limited. Access must follow non-burst MMIO rules. Sample counters periodically and correlate with other logs.
dependencies: Use only in low-impact windows; MMIO helpers
Remarks: Gate heavy diagnostics behind service modes to avoid impact.

description: An I/O space window is provided for legacy device access in RC mode. The aperture is small and should be used cautiously. Many modern endpoints prefer memory-mapped access. Access rules still require single 32-bit operations. Validate device capabilities before relying on I/O space.
dependencies: RC mode; endpoints advertise I/O BARs
Remarks: Fallback to memory space if I/O BARs are absent or problematic.

description: Multiple virtual channels or traffic classes beyond defaults are not supported. Function-level reset is not implemented. Software must not advertise or depend on these capabilities. Negotiation during enumeration should reflect these limits. Attempting to use them can cause interoperability issues.
dependencies: Constrain capability advertisement; clear unsupported bits
Remarks: Keep capability advertisement minimal for broad compatibility.
